title: NOC 64401 – Postal services representatives | GTR Immigration
description: Postal services representatives under NOC 64401 are the essential frontline operators of Canada’s logistics and communication networks. Working primarily within Canada Post outlets and private courier establishments, these TEER 4 professionals manage the complex exchange of letters, parcels, and financial products like money orders. As e-commerce continues to drive record-breaking parcel volumes in 2026, this role offers high job stability and serves as a reliable entry point for international workers into the Canadian service and logistics sectors.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level4Major Group64 – Sales and service representativesSub-Major Group644 – Customer and information services representativesMinor Group6440 – Customer and information services representativesNOC Code64401

duties: Calculate and process postage for letters, parcels, and specialized mail (Registered, Express, etc.).
Sell postage stamps, prepaid envelopes, and various postal/courier supplies.
Process financial transactions, including money orders, utility bill payments, and cash-on-delivery (COD) items.
Provide information to the public regarding postal regulations, rates, and delivery schedules.
Maintain records of inventory and handle customer inquiries or complaints regarding lost or delayed mail.
Sort and file mail according to destination when required.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Several weeks of on-the-job training are typically provided by the employer.
Basic mathematical and computer skills are mandatory.
High proficiency in English or French (or both) is essential for customer interaction.
A security clearance check is often required for those handling sensitive mail and financial products.
job_outlook: The outlook for this occupation is Favourable. Projections indicate that as Canada’s population grows and e-commerce infrastructure expands, the need for retail-facing postal representatives will remain steady. Retirements of long-term employees will also create significant entry-level openings.
labour_market_demand: The demand for postal services representatives is Stable to Good. While digital communication has reduced letter mail, the exponential growth of online shopping has made parcel handling a critical national priority. Demand is highest in rapidly growing suburban areas and major logistics hubs like Mississauga (Ontario), Richmond (BC), and Calgary (Alberta).
Essential Service Stability: Work in a sector that remains fully operational regardless of economic fluctuations.
Unionized Environments: Many positions (especially with Canada Post) offer strong job security, competitive wages, and comprehensive benefits.
Logistics Foundation: Gain experience in the Canadian supply chain, providing a path toward Logistics Supervision (NOC 12013).
pathways: BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled: A pathway for those working in specific service sectors (requires 9 months of work in BC).
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Fast-track for those already working in Alberta in this category with a valid job offer.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A direct route for TEER 4 workers with job offers in the Maritimes.
CEC (Canadian Experience Class): Accessible after completing 12 months of full-time work in Canada on a valid permit.
